overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a strong, multi-platform professional presence with a total follower base of 3,580. His content highly resonates within the academic and computational social science communities, focusing on disinformation, coordinated behavior, and research opportunities. Engagement is robust across all channels, driven by milestone announcements such as new publications, software releases, and hiring opportunities.

contentThemes.description: Discussion and research updates on coordinated link sharing, CooRnet software updates, and platform-wide disinformation campaigns.
Recruitment posts for postdoc positions and research opportunities for PhD students in computational social science.
Announcements of newly published papers, research grants, and upcoming conference presentations.

recommendations: Increase posting frequency on LinkedIn to maximize the reach of its larger follower base (1,850 followers).
Leverage Bluesky's high interaction rates by initiating discussions and asking open-ended questions related to research methodology.
Include direct links to publications and the CooRnet tool on Mastodon and Bluesky to convert engagement into platform traffic.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky represents the most engaging platform with an average engagement rate of 6.14%, showing a strong community response to academic opportunities and publication announcements.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon provides steady engagement (4.55% average rate) particularly for software updates like CooRnet, serving as an effective channel for reaching niche technical audiences.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn generates the highest absolute volume of engagement, proving highly effective for professional announcements like research grants (107 interactions) and postdoctoral recruitment (73 interactions).

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
